senses.definition: a sport that involves various athletic events, including running, jumping, and throwing competitions, that are held on a running track

examples.x: She excels in both sprints and long-distance events in track and field.
The team’s track and field coach emphasized strength and agility.
Track and field athletes train rigorously for months before a competition.
The school’s track and field team won several medals at the state championships.

senses.definition: a tough triathlon race where athletes swim, bike, and run long distances without stopping

examples.x: Ironman events attract athletes from around the world seeking the ultimate endurance challenge.

senses.definition: the sport of running, riding, skiing, or driving over natural terrain
a long-distance running race covering a distance of 21.0975 kilometres
a running race of 26 miles or 42 kilometers

examples.x: She trained for months to run her first marathon.
He completed the marathon in just under four hours.
A sudden injury forced her to withdraw from the marathon.
The city hosts an annual marathon every spring.
Running a marathon requires endurance and dedication.

senses.definition: a long-distance running race longer than the traditional marathon distance of 42.195 kilometers

examples.x: She trained for months to prepare for her first ultramarathon.
He completed an ultramarathon spanning 100 kilometers in the mountains.
The ultramarathon took runners through challenging terrain and extreme weather conditions.
Participants in the ultramarathon faced steep inclines and rocky trails.
Sarah set a new personal record in the ultramarathon, finishing in under ten hours.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of moving through an area, particularly an urban area, by running, jumping, and climbing over, under, or around different obstacles

examples.x: Urban landscapes provide diverse challenges for parkour enthusiasts, who navigate obstacles creatively and efficiently.
Safety and technique are paramount in parkour training to prevent injuries and improve agility.
Videos showcasing skilled parkour athletes performing impressive stunts often go viral on social media platforms.

senses.definition: a high-intensity fitness program that combines various exercises like weightlifting, cardio, and gymnastics

examples.x: She joined a CrossFit gym to challenge herself and improve her overall fitness level, pushing through intense workouts that combined strength training and conditioning.
CrossFit workouts often involve a variety of functional movements performed at high intensity, such as squats, deadlifts, burpees, and kettlebell swings.
The CrossFit community fosters a supportive and competitive atmosphere, with members encouraging each other to push beyond their limits and achieve their fitness goals.
CrossFit has gained popularity in recent years for its effectiveness in improving strength, endurance, and overall athletic performance, attracting athletes of all ages and fitness levels.

senses.definition: the action of competing in an athletic event where participants move swiftly on foot, typically on a track

examples.x: She achieved a personal best time in the 800-meter running event.

senses.definition: athletic competitions specifically designed for athletes with physical disabilities

examples.x: She aspires to represent her country in para-athletics at the upcoming Paralympic Games.
Para-athletics encompasses a wide range of sports adapted for individuals with disabilities.
His success in para-athletics has inspired others with disabilities to pursue their athletic dreams.
The growth of para-athletics has led to increased participation among athletes with physical disabilities.
His dedication to para-athletics earned him recognition as a Paralympic champion.

senses.definition: a multisport event comprising swimming, cycling, and running for athletes with physical disabilities

examples.x: She trained rigorously for the para triathlon, focusing on all three disciplines.
He set a new personal best time in the para triathlon, earning him a spot on the podium.
His dedication to para triathlon has inspired many others with disabilities to take up the sport.
The para triathlon consists of swimming, cycling, and running segments.
The para triathlon is a challenging test of endurance and skill for athletes with physical disabilities.

senses.definition: a competition consisting of ten different sports that takes place over two days

examples.x: He trained tirelessly for the decathlon, preparing for the grueling schedule of events that included sprinting, jumping, throwing, and endurance races.
The decathlon requires a diverse skill set and athleticism, testing competitors' speed, strength, agility, and stamina across a range of disciplines.
She won the gold medal in the decathlon at the Olympic Games, earning recognition as the world's greatest all-around athlete.
The decathlon competition began with the 100-meter dash, followed by the long jump, shot put, high jump, and 400-meter race on the first day.
He struggled with fatigue during the final events of the decathlon but summoned the strength to finish strong and earn a podium spot.

senses.definition: a combined event consisting of seven track and field events for female athletes

examples.x: The heptathlon includes events such as the 100m hurdles and high jump.
Her strongest event in the heptathlon is the javelin throw.
The heptathlon is a grueling test of athleticism and endurance.
She broke the national record in the heptathlon with an outstanding performance.

senses.definition: an athletic competition with five different events

examples.x: The pentathlon includes running, swimming, fencing, equestrian, and shooting.
He won the gold medal in the pentathlon at the Olympics.
Jane is training hard for the upcoming pentathlon.

senses.definition: a multi-discipline event consisting of four components: running, swimming, shooting, and horse riding

examples.x: The tetrathlon challenges athletes both physically and mentally.
Participants in the tetrathlon must transition smoothly between each discipline.
He struggled with the shooting component of the tetrathlon but excelled in the other events.
The final event of the tetrathlon is the horse riding competition.
The tetrathlon requires athletes to excel in running, swimming, shooting, and horse riding.

senses.definition: the athletic action or sport of pushing oneself off the ground using one or both legs, often for height or distance

examples.x: Jumping requires strength and timing.
Jumping is both a skill and a test of athleticism in sports.

senses.definition: a track and field event in which an athlete uses a long, flexible pole to jump over a bar

examples.x: The athlete cleared the bar with ease during the pole vault competition.
He set a new personal record in pole vault at the track meet.
She trained for months to improve her pole vault technique.
The pole vault is one of the most exciting events to watch in track and field.

senses.definition: a sport in which participants jump over a horizontal bar that is placed at different heights without using any equipment
an athletic event in which competitors run down a track and jump as far as possible from a takeoff board, aiming to land in a pit filled with sand
an athletic event in which competitors take three consecutive jumps, consisting of a hop, step, and jump, in order to achieve the greatest distance possible

examples.x: The athlete practiced his triple jump technique every day to improve his distance.
She managed to land a solid hop, step, and jump in the triple jump event, setting a new personal record.
During the competition, his triple jump was impressive, covering a long distance on each phase.
He was disappointed with his triple jump result, as he had hoped to break the school record.

senses.definition: the events where participants compete by hurling objects such as javelins, shot puts, or discuses for distance or accuracy

examples.x: Javelin throw is a popular throwing sport in track and field competitions.
His coach specializes in training athletes for various throwing sports.
The throwing sport requires a combination of strength, technique, and precision.
The throwing sport competitions feature athletes from all over the world showcasing their skills.
He broke the world record in the javelin throw, showcasing his mastery in the throwing sport.

senses.definition: an athletic event where athletes spin and release a heavy metal ball attached to a wire and handle, aiming to achieve the longest distance possible

examples.x: He trained tirelessly to perfect his technique in the hammer throw.
She won a gold medal in the hammer throw at the national championships.
He adjusted his grip to maximize his distance in the hammer throw.
The hammer throw requires strength, coordination, and precise timing.
He competed against top athletes from around the world in the hammer throw.

senses.definition: a field event where athletes hurl a heavy discus as far as possible within a designated throwing area

examples.x: She focused on her footwork to enhance her discus throw distance.
The discus throw event requires strength, technique, and precision.
Her discus throw set a new personal best at the competition.
They measured the distance of each discus throw carefully to determine the winner.
His consistent discus throw performances earned him a spot on the national team.

senses.definition: an athletic field event where competitors throw a heavy metal ball as far as possible within a marked circle

examples.x: She practiced her shot put technique every day to improve her strength and distance.
The shot put competition was the final event of the track meet.
His personal best in shot put is 15 meters, a great achievement for him.
The coach gave him tips on how to improve his shot put throw by using his whole body.
The shot put area is marked off with a special line to measure each athlete's throw.

senses.definition: a sport or competition in which a light spear is thrown as far as possible
the athletic event or technique in track and field where athletes sprint and jump over hurdles set at regular distances along a track
a race in which athletes run a distance of 400 meters while clearing hurdles set along the track

examples.x: She trained tirelessly for the 400 meters hurdles event, focusing on both speed and technique.
The athlete stumbled at the fifth hurdle during the 400 meters hurdles race.
His personal best in the 400 meters hurdles earned him a spot on the national team.
The 400 meters hurdles final featured some of the world's top athletes.
She cleared each hurdle smoothly in the 400 meters hurdles, maintaining her lead.

senses.definition: a race where athletes sprint over a series of hurdles spaced 110 meters apart on a track

examples.x: He specializes in the 110 meters hurdles and holds several national records.
The final of the 110 meters hurdles will take place tomorrow afternoon.
She stumbled at the third hurdle during the 110 meters hurdles race.
The athlete cleared each hurdle smoothly in the 110 meters hurdles event.
She won the gold medal in the 110 meters hurdles at the national championships.

senses.definition: a short-distance sprinting race in track and field, typically ranging from 60 meters to 400 meters

examples.x: She won the 100-meter dash at the regional championships.
Spectators lined the track to watch the exciting dash races.
He sprinted to victory in the final stretch of the 60-meter dash.
His explosive speed made him a formidable competitor in the dash.
The dash requires quick acceleration and strong finishing speed.

senses.definition: the competitions involving jumping, throwing, or other activities performed on a field, rather than running events
to move faster in comparison to other things or people

examples.x: The athlete outstripped all his competitors in the final race.
Sales of electric cars have outstripped traditional gasoline vehicles.
The rabbit quickly outstripped the slower animals in the race.
Demand for the new product outstripped the company’s supply.
The spaceship outstripped all previous speed records.

senses.definition: an individual's highest achievement or performance in a particular activity or event

examples.x: She set a new personal best in the 100-meter sprint, shaving off milliseconds from her previous time.
His personal best in weightlifting earned him a gold medal at the competition.
After months of training, he finally surpassed his personal best in long jump.
Setting a personal best in the marathon was a remarkable accomplishment for her.
His personal best in the high jump was unmatched by any of his competitors.

senses.definition: the designated point on a track or field where athletes begin a race or event

examples.x: Athletes lined up at the starting line, ready for the race to begin.
The starting line is crucial for a strong start in track events.
He positioned himself carefully at the starting line for the 100-meter sprint.
Spectators watched as the runners took their positions at the starting line.
The official signaled the athletes to get set at the starting line.

senses.definition: a competitive athletic event held on a designated track, involving various disciplines such as sprints, hurdles, middle-distance, and long-distance running, as well as relays

examples.x: She excelled in the 800-meter track event, winning by a wide margin.
His training focused on improving his speed for upcoming track events.
Spectators gathered to watch the exciting track events at the stadium.
He competed in multiple track events throughout the track and field season.
She set a new personal record in the 1500-meter track event.

senses.definition: an athletic race in track and field where teams of runners compete by sequentially passing a baton from one teammate to the next over a set distance

examples.x: His team won the 4x100 meter relay at the championship meet.
She anchored the relay team, bringing them from behind to victory.
She passed the baton smoothly during the relay exchange zone.
Relay events showcase both individual speed and team coordination.
The relay is a highly anticipated event in track and field competitions.

senses.definition: a race where participants move swiftly on foot to reach a specific distance or finish line

examples.x: He won the 400-meter run at the track meet, setting a new school record.
He placed third in the 10K charity run, raising funds for cancer research.

senses.definition: a track and field event where athletes jump forward from a standing position, aiming for distance rather than height

examples.x: He set a new personal record in the broad jump at the track meet.
The athlete's broad jump technique helped him win the gold medal.
Participants in the decathlon compete in events like the broad jump.
She practiced her approach for the broad jump during training.
The broad jump event is also known as the long jump in some regions.

senses.definition: a race in which people or animals, typically horses, have to jump over fences, ditches, bushes, etc. in order to finish the race

examples.x: The annual steeplechase attracted a large crowd, eager to watch the horses and riders navigate the challenging course of fences and water jumps.
She trained rigorously for the steeplechase, focusing on her speed and agility to successfully clear each obstacle in the race.
The steeplechase was one of the most exciting events at the track and field competition, with athletes leaping over barriers and splashing through water pits.
The steeplechase course was designed to test the endurance and skill of both the horse and rider, featuring a variety of jumps and turns.
He excelled in the steeplechase, consistently finishing at the top of his class due to his powerful running and precise jumping abilities.
